briefdescription.para: A connect event interrupt type.
Interrupt type for bridge verflow.
Interrupt type for AUX peripheral.
Interrupt type for AUD peripheral.
Interrupt type for LNKSTA peripheral.
Interrupt type for DDC peripheral.
Interrupt type for stream down.
Interrupt type for stream init.
Interrupt type for stream up.
Interrupt type for hdcp.
Interrupt type for HDCP14PROT event.
Interrupt type for HDCP22PROT event.
Interrupt type for link error.
Interrupt type for sync loss.
Interrupt type for mode.
Interrupt type for TMDS clock ratio.
Interrupt type for VIC error.
Handler for Configuration Retry Request.
Interrupt type for Link Ready error.
Interrupt type for Video Ready error.
Interrupt type for Skew Lock error.
Handler for FRL Config.
Handler for FRL Start.
Handler for TMDS.
Handler for VFP change event.
Handler for VRR rdy event.
Handler for Dynamic HDR.
Handler type for DSC stream change event.
Handler type for DSC PPS Packet error event.
Handler type for SCDC Reg 0x10 bit 0 Status_Update bit set by HDMI Source.
These constants specify different types of handler and used to differentiate interrupt requests from peripheral.
Stream down.
Stream idle.
Stream init.
Stream arm.
Stream lock.
Stream ready.
Stream up.
These constants specify different types of stream states.
Sync Loss.
Sync Lock.
These constants specifies the sync lock status.
These constants defines the various audio format types that can be detected or processed by the HDMI RX subsystem.
These constants defines the supported EDID RAM sizes for the HDMI RX subsystem.

sectiondef.header: HDMI RX Dynamic HDR Error type

briefdescription.para: Sequence id or ECC error.
Memory write error.
This enumeration defines the possible error conditions that may occur while handling Dynamic HDR metadata in the HDMI RX subsystem.
Prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros.
Represents a 10 millisecond time interval in clock cycles.

detaileddescription.para: Calculated based on the AXI Lite interface frequency. Useful for short delays or polling intervals.

briefdescription.para: Represents a 200 millisecond time interval in clock cycles.

detaileddescription.para: Useful for medium-duration timeouts or periodic checks.

briefdescription.para: Represents a 16 millisecond time interval in clock cycles.

detaileddescription.para: Equivalent to approximately one frame at 60 Hz refresh rate. Useful for frame-based timing.

briefdescription.para: Represents a 500 millisecond time interval in clock cycles.

detaileddescription.para: Suitable for longer delays or watchdog timers.

briefdescription.para: This macro returns the clock cycles required to count up to 10Ms with respect to AXI Lite Frequency.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRX1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro returns the clock cycles required to count up to 16Ms with respect to AXI Lite Frequency.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRX1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro returns the clock cycles required to count up to 200Ms with respect to AXI Lite Frequency.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRX1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro returns the clock cycles required to count up to 1s with respect to AXI Lite Frequency.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRX1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro reads the RX version.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XHdmi_RX core instance.
RX version. *note C-style signature: u32 XV_HdmiRx1_GetVersion(XV_HdmiRx1 *InstancePtr)

briefdescription.para: This macro asserts or clears the HDMI RX reset.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either assert or release HDMI RX reset.

simplesect.para: The reset output of the PIO is inverted. When the system is in reset, the PIO output is cleared and this will reset the HDMI RX. Therefore, clearing the PIO reset output will assert the HDMI link and video reset. C-style signature: void XV_HdmiRx1_Reset(XV_HdmiRx1 *InstancePtr, u8 Reset)

briefdescription.para: This macro asserts or clears the HDMI RX link enable.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either assert or release HDMI RX link enable.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XV_HdmiRx1_Reset(XV_HdmiRx1 *InstancePtr, u8 SetClr)

briefdescription.para: This macro asserts or clears the HDMI RX video enable.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either assert or release HDMI RX video enable.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XV_HdmiRx1_Reset(XV_HdmiRx1 *InstancePtr, u8 SetClr)

briefdescription.para: This macro controls the HDMI RX Scrambler.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Rx core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either enable or disable the scrambler.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XV_HdmiRx1_SetScrambler(XV_HdmiRx1 *InstancePtr, u8 SetClr)

briefdescription.para: This macro controls the YUV420 mode for video bridge.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Rx core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either enable or disable the YUV 420 Support.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XV_HdmiRx1_Bridge_yuv420(XV_HdmiRx1 *InstancePtr, u8 SetClr)

briefdescription.para: This macro controls the Pixel Drop mode for video bridge.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Rx core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either enable or disable the Pixel Repetition.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XV_HdmiRx1_Bridge_pixel(XV_HdmiRx1 *InstancePtr, u8 SetClr)

briefdescription.para: This macro asserts or clears the AXIS enable output port.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either enable or disable the AXIS output port.

simplesect.para: The reset output of the PIO is inverted. When the system is in reset, the PIO output is cleared and this will reset the HDMI RX. Therefore, clearing the PIO reset output will assert the HDMI link and video reset. C-style signature: void XV_HdmiRx1_AxisEnable(InstancePtr, Enable)

briefdescription.para: This macro enables the HDMI RX PIO peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables the HDMI RX PIO peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables interrupts in the HDMI RX PIO peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables interrupts in the HDMI RX PIO peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables the HDMI RX timer peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables the HDMI RX timer peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables interrupts in the HDMI RX timer peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables interrupt in the HDMI RX timer peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro starts the HDMI RX timer peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.
specifies the timer's frequency in number of clockcycles

briefdescription.para: This macro reads the HDMI RX timer peripheral's remaining timer counter value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables the HDMI RX timer peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables the HDMI RX timer peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables interrupts in the HDMI RX timer peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables interrupt in the HDMI RX timer peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro starts the HDMI RX timer peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.
specifies the timer's frequency in number of clockcycles

briefdescription.para: This macro reads the HDMI RX timer peripheral's remaining timer counter value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables the HDMI RX timer peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables the HDMI RX timer peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables interrupts in the HDMI RX timer peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables interrupt in the HDMI RX timer peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro starts the HDMI RX timer peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.
specifies the timer's frequency in number of clockcycles

briefdescription.para: This macro reads the HDMI RX timer peripheral's remaining timer counter value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables the HDMI RX timer peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables the HDMI RX timer peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables interrupts in the HDMI RX timer peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables interrupt in the HDMI RX timer peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro starts the HDMI RX timer peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.
specifies the timer's frequency in number of clockcycles

briefdescription.para: This macro reads the HDMI RX timer peripheral's remaining timer counter value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables the HDMI RX Timing Detector peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables the HDMI RX Timing Detector peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables interrupt in the HDMI RX Timing Detector peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables interrupt in the HDMI RX Timing Detector peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro allow control to enable/disable the HDMI RX VFP event.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either enable or disable the VFP Event

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XV_HdmiRx1_VtdVfpEvent(XV_HdmiRx1 *InstancePtr, u8 SetClr)

briefdescription.para: This macro sets the timebase in the HDMI RX Timing Detector peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.
specifies the timer's frequency in number of clockcycles

briefdescription.para: This macro enables the HDMI RX Display Data Channel (DDC) peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables the SCDC in the DDC peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables the HDCP in the DDC peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables the HDCP in the DDC peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro sets the DDC peripheral into HDCP 1.4 mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro sets the DDC peripheral into HDCP 2.2 mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables the HDMI RX Display Data Channel (DDC) peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables interrupts in the HDMI RX Display Data Channel (DDC) peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables interrupts in the HDMI RX Display Data Channel (DDC) peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

memberdef.name: XV_HdmiRx1_DdcScdcClear
memberdef.initializer: { \ XV_HdmiRx1_WriteReg((InstancePtr)->Config.BaseAddress, \ (XV_HDMIRX1_DDC_CTRL_SET_OFFSET), \ (XV_HDMIRX1_DDC_CTRL_SCDC_CLR_MASK)); \ usleep(50);\ XV_HdmiRx1_WriteReg((InstancePtr)->Config.BaseAddress, \ (XV_HDMIRX1_DDC_CTRL_CLR_OFFSET), \ (XV_HDMIRX1_DDC_CTRL_SCDC_CLR_MASK)); \ /* FRL-specific SCDC field writes */ \ XV_HdmiRx1_FrlDdcWriteField(InstancePtr, XV_HDMIRX1_SCDCFIELD_FLT_READY,\ 0);\ XV_HdmiRx1_FrlDdcWriteField(InstancePtr,XV_HDMIRX1_SCDCFIELD_SINK_VER, \ 1);\ }

briefdescription.para: This macro clears the SCDC registers in the DDC peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables the HDMI RX Auxiliary (AUX) peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables the HDMI RX Auxiliary (AUX) peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables interrupts in the HDMI RX Auxiliary (AUX) peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables interrupts in the HDMI RX Auxiliary (AUX) peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables FSync/VRR event interrupt in the HDMI RX Auxiliary (AUX) peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables FSync/VRR event interrupt in the HDMI RX Auxiliary (AUX) peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables the HDMI RX Audio (AUD) peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables the HDMI RX Audio (AUD) peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables interrupts in the HDMI RX Audio (AUD) peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables interrupts in the HDMI RX Audio (AUD) peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables ACR Update Event in the HDMI RX Audio (AUD) peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables ACR Update Event in the HDMI RX Audio (AUD) peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables the HDMI RX Link Status (LNKSTA) peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables the HDMI RX Link Status (LNKSTA) peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables interrupt in the HDMI RX Link Status (LNKSTA) peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disable interrupt in the HDMI RX Link Status (LNKSTA) peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro returns true is the audio stream is active else false.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

simplesect.para: TRUE if the audio stream is active, FALSE if it is not.

briefdescription.para: This macro returns the number of active audio channels.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro clears the HDCP write message buffer in the DDC peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Rx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro clears the HDCP read message buffer in the DDC peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Rx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro enables the data mover for Dynamic HDR.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro disables the data mover for Dynamic HDR.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: Callback type for interrupt.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.

briefdescription.para: Callback type for interrupt.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.
is a type of HDCP event

briefdescription.para: This function returns a reference to an XV_HdmiRx1_Config structure based on the core id, DeviceId.

detaileddescription.para: The return value will refer to an entry in the device configuration table defined in the xv_hdmirx1_g.c file.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique core ID of the HDMI RX core for the lookup operation.

simplesect.para: XV_HdmiRx1_LookupConfig returns a reference to a config record in the configuration table (in xv_hdmirx1_g.c) corresponding to DeviceId, or NULL if no match is found.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the HDMI RX core.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called prior to using the HDMI RX core. Initialization of the HDMI RX includes setting up the instance data, and ensuring the hardware is in a quiescent state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiRx1 core instance.
points to the configuration structure associated with the HDMI RX core.
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if XV_HdmiRx1_CfgInitialize was successful.
XST_FAILURE if HDMI RX PIO ID mismatched.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the AXI4-Lite Clock Frequency.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.
specifies the value that needs to be set.

simplesect.para: This is required after a reset or init.

briefdescription.para: This function clears the HDMI RX variables and sets them to the defaults.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

simplesect.para: This is required after a reset or init.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the HDMI RX stream parameters.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

specifies the pixel per clock.
4 = XVIDC_PPC_4
specifies reference pixel clock frequency.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS is always returned.

briefdescription.para: This function provides status of the stream.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

listitem.para: TRUE = Stream is up.
FALSE = Stream is down.

briefdescription.para: This function provides the stream scrambler status.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

listitem.para: TRUE = Stream is scrambled.
FALSE = Stream is not scrambled.

briefdescription.para: This function provides the stream connected status.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

listitem.para: TRUE = Stream is connected.
FALSE = Stream is connected.

briefdescription.para: This function enables/clear Hot-Plug-Detect.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either enable or clear HPD respectively.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS is always returned.

briefdescription.para: This function asserts or releases the HDMI RX Internal VRST.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either assert or release HDMI RX Internal VRST.

simplesect.para: The reset output of the PIO is inverted. When the system is in reset, the PIO output is cleared and this will reset the HDMI RX. Therefore, clearing the PIO reset output will assert the HDMI Internal video reset. C-style signature: void XV_HdmiRx1_INT_VRST(XV_HdmiRx1 *InstancePtr, u8 Reset)

briefdescription.para: This function asserts or releases the HDMI RX Internal LRST.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either assert or release HDMI RX Internal LRST.

simplesect.para: The reset output of the PIO is inverted. When the system is in reset, the PIO output is cleared and this will reset the HDMI RX. Therefore, clearing the PIO reset output will assert the HDMI Internal link reset. C-style signature: void XV_HdmiRx1_INT_VRST(XV_HdmiRx1 *InstancePtr, u8 Reset)

briefdescription.para: This function asserts or releases the HDMI RX External VRST.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either assert or release HDMI RX External VRST.

simplesect.para: The reset output of the PIO is inverted. When the system is in reset, the PIO output is cleared and this will reset the HDMI RX. Therefore, clearing the PIO reset output will assert the HDMI external video reset. C-style signature: void XV_HdmiRx1_EXT_VRST(XV_HdmiRx1 *InstancePtr, u8 Reset)

briefdescription.para: This function asserts or releases the HDMI RX External SYSRST.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either assert or release HDMI RX External SYSRST.

simplesect.para: The reset output of the PIO is inverted. When the system is in reset, the PIO output is cleared and this will reset the HDMI RX. Therefore, clearing the PIO reset output will assert the HDMI External system reset. C-style signature: void XV_HdmiRx1_EXT_SYSRST(XV_HdmiRx1 *InstancePtr, u8 Reset)

briefdescription.para: This function sets the pixel rate.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS is always returned.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the color format.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function provides status of one of the link error counters reached the maximum value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

listitem.para: TRUE = Maximum error counter reached.
FALSE = Maximum error counter not reached.

briefdescription.para: This function clears the link error counters.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function provides status of the HDMI RX core Link Status peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

specifies one of the type for which status to be provided:
0 = Link error counter for channel 0.
1 = Link error counter for channel 1.
2 = Link error counter for channel 2.

simplesect.para: Link status of the HDMI RX core link.

briefdescription.para: This function provides audio clock regenerating CTS (Cycle-Time Stamp) value at the HDMI sink device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function provides audio clock regenerating factor N value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function loads the EDID data into the DDC slave.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.
is a pointer to the EDID data array.
is the length, in bytes, of the EDID array.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the EDID data was loaded successfully
XST_FAILURE if the EDID data load failed

briefdescription.para: This function sets the HDCP address in the DDC peripheral.

detaileddescription.para: This is implemented as a function and not a macro, so the HDCP driver can bind the function call with a handler.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Rx core instance.
is the HDCP address.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XHdmiRx1_DdcHdcpSetAddress(XHdmi_Rx *InstancePtr, u8 Address)

briefdescription.para: This function writes HDCP data in the DDC peripheral.

detaileddescription.para: This is implemented as a function and not a macro, so the HDCP driver can bind the function call with a handler.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Rx core instance.
is the HDCP data to be written.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XHdmiRx1_DdcHdcpWriteData(XHdmi_Rx *InstancePtr, u8 Data)

briefdescription.para: This function reads HDCP data from the DDC peripheral.

detaileddescription.para: This is implemented as a function and not a macro, so the HDCP driver can bind the function call with a handler.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Rx core instance.

simplesect.para: Returns the HDCP data read from the DDC peripheral.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the number of bytes of the HDCP 2.2 write buffer in the DDC slave.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Rx core instance.

listitem.para: HDCP 2.2 write buffer words

briefdescription.para: This function returns the status of the HDCP 2.2 write buffer in the DDC slave.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Rx core instance.

listitem.para: TRUE = HDCP 2.2 message buffer is empty.
FALSE = HDCP 2.2 message buffer contains data.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the number of bytes of the HDCP 2.2 read buffer in the DDC slave.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Rx core instance.

listitem.para: HDCP 2.2 read buffer words

briefdescription.para: This function returns the status of the HDCP 2.2 read message buffer in the DDC slave.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Rx core instance.

listitem.para: TRUE = HDCP 2.2 message buffer is empty.
FALSE = HDCP 2.2 message buffer contains data.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the SCDC TMDS clock ratio bit.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

listitem.para: TRUE = TMDS clock ratio bit is set.
FALSE = TMDS clock ratio bit is cleared.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the AVI VIC (captured by the AUX peripheral)

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the AVI colorspace (captured by the AUX peripheral)

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the GCP color depth (captured by the AUX peripheral)

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the video properties from the aux peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the video timing from the VTD peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

simplesect.para: Calculates video timing and returns video timing parameters structure

briefdescription.para: This function calculates the divider for the frame calculation.

parameterdescription.para: is the dividend value to use in the calculation.
is the divisor value to use in the calculation.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the PixelClk based on the current ColorDepth, RefClk and ColorFormatId.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function starts the HDMI RX core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

simplesect.para: This is required after a reset or initialization.

briefdescription.para: This function stops the HDMI RX core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if RX's CED or RSED counters are incrementing at the rate of 4 or higher per second or if they first hit the maximum value (0x7FFF) then set the CED_Update or RSED_Update SCDC flags if true.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

simplesect.para: This function needs to be called every 1 second to comply with the spec on CED_Update and RSED_Update flags updating.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the timer of RX Core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Rx core instance.
specifies the timer's frequency (in milliseconds)
selects which of the timer unit to be used

briefdescription.para: This function searches for the video mode based on the vic.

simplesect.para: Vic defined in the VIC table.

briefdescription.para: This function Parses the Source Product Descriptor (SPD) InfoFrame to extract FreeSync and FreeSync Pro information.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the VTEM (Video Timing Extended Metadatai) InfoFrame data from the HDMI RX auxiliary register and populates the corresponding fields in the XV_HdmiC_VideoTimingExtMeta structure.

detaileddescription.para: The VTEM InfoFrame provides metadata for features like Variable Refresh Rate (VRR), Quick Media Switching (QMS), and other advanced timing parameters.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the specified FRL SCDC Field.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.
specifies the fields from SCDC channels to be written

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS
XST_FAILURE

briefdescription.para: This function writes the specified FRL SCDC Field.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.
specifies the fields from SCDC channels to be written
specifies the values to be written

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS
XST_FAILURE
XST_DEVICE_BUSY

briefdescription.para: This function initializes FRL-specific SCDC fields that are required for FRL operation.

detaileddescription.para: This should be called separately from the basic SCDC clear operation when FRL is enabled.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

simplesect.para: This function should only be called when FRL is enabled.

briefdescription.para: This function prints stream and timing information on STDIO/UART console.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function prints debug information on STDIO/UART console.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function prints out HDMI RX register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function prints out RX's SCDC registers and values on STDIO/UART.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function reads ID of PIO peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HDMI RX core instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if PIO ID was matched.
XST_FAILURE if PIO ID was mismatched.

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the HDMI RX driver.

detaileddescription.para: This handler reads the pending interrupt from PIO, DDC, TIMDET, AUX, AUD and LNKSTA peripherals, determines the source of the interrupts, clears the interrupts and calls callbacks accordingly.

The application is responsible for connecting this function to the interrupt system. Application beyond this driver is also responsible for providing callbacks to handle interrupts and installing the callbacks using XV_HdmiRx1_SetCallback() during initialization phase. An example delivered with this driver demonstrates how this could be done.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx1 instance that just interrupted.

briefdescription.para: This function installs an asynchronous callback function for the given HandlerType:

para.preformatted: HandlerType Callback Function Type ------------------------- ----------------------------------------------- (XV_HDMIRX1_HANDLER_VTD) VtdCallback (XV_HDMIRX1_HANDLER_AUX) AuxCallback (XV_HDMIRX1_HANDLER_AUD) AudCallback (XV_HDMIRX1_HANDLER_LNKSTA) LnkStaCallback (XV_HDMIRX1_HANDLER_PIO) PioCallback

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the HDMI RX core instance.
specifies the type of handler.
is the address of the callback function.
is a user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if callback function installed successfully.
XST_INVALID_PARAM when HandlerType is invalid.

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.

briefdescription.para: This function returns Video Timing Extended Metadata.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns AMD Source Product Descriptor Infoframe.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns VRR infoframe type.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiRx1 core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function Sets VRR infoframe type.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiRx1 core instance.
of type XV_HdmiRx1_VrrInfoframeType

briefdescription.para: This function sets the Dynamic HDR buffer address.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiRx1 core instance.
is an address in 64bit format.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the Dynamic HDR packet type, length, whether graphics overlay and errors if any.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiRx1 core instance.
is a pointer to XHdmiRx1 Dynamic HDR info instance.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if Display Stream Compression (DSC) is enabled on the HDMI RX stream.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiRx1 core instance.

listitem.para: 1 if DSC is enabled on the stream.
0 if DSC is not enabled or not supported.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the DSC Decode Fail flag in the HDMI RX FRL DDC field.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiRx1 core instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the operation was successful.
XST_NO_FEATURE if DSC is not supported in the current configuration

simplesect.para: This function uses an assertion to ensure that the input pointer is not NULL.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the DSC FRL Max flag in the HDMI RX FRL DDC field.

detaileddescription.para: This function writes a value of 1 to the DSC_FRL_MAX field using the FRL DDC interface. It is used to indicate that the maximum FRL (Fixed Rate Link) bandwidth is being used for Display Stream Compression (DSC) on the HDMI RX path

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiRx1 core instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the operation was successful.
XST_NO_FEATURE if DSC is not supported in the current configuration

simplesect.para: This function uses an assertion to ensure that the input pointer is not NULL.

briefdescription.para: This is the main header file for Xilinx HDMI RX core.

detaileddescription.para:
HDMI RX core is used for extracting the video and audio streams from HDMI stream. It consists of
Receiver core
AXI4-Stream to Video Bridge
Video Timing Controller and
High-bandwidth Digital Content Protection (HDCP) (Optional)
Data Recovery Unit (DRU) (Optional).

Receiver core performs following operations:
Aligns incoming data stream to the word boundary and removes inter channel skew.
Unscrambles the data if data rates above the 3.4 Gps. Otherwise bypasses the Scrambler.
Splits the data stream into video and packet data streams.
Optional data streams decrypt by an external HDCP module.
Decodes TMDS data into video data.
Converts the pixel data from the link domain into the video domain.
AXI Video Bridge converts the captured native video to AXI stream and outputs the video data through the AXI video interface.
Video Timing Controller (VTC) measures the video timing.
Data Recovery Unit (DRU) to recover the data from the HDMI stream if incoming HDMI stream is too slow for the transceiver.
For a full description of HDMI RX features, please see the hardware specification.
The application needs to do following steps in order for preparing the HDMI RX core to be ready.

listitem.para: Call XV_HdmiRx1_LookupConfig using a device ID to find the core configuration.
Call XV_HdmiRx1_CfgInitialize to initialize the device and the driver instance associated with it.

detaileddescription.para:
This driver provides interrupt handlers
XV_HdmiRx1_IntrHandler, for handling the interrupts from the HDMI RX core peripherals.

Application developer needs to register interrupt handler with the processor, within their examples. Whenever processor calls registered application's interrupt handler associated with interrupt id, application's interrupt handler needs to call appropriate peripheral interrupt handler reading peripheral's Status register.
This driver provides XV_HdmiRx1_SetCallback API to register functions with HDMI RX core instance.
This driver supports Virtual Memory. The RTOS is responsible for calculating the correct device base address in Virtual Memory space.
This driver is not thread safe. Any needs for threads or thread mutual exclusion must be satisfied by the layer above this driver.

Asserts are used within all Xilinx drivers to enforce constraints on argument values. Asserts can be turned off on a system-wide basis by defining, at compile time, the NDEBUG identifier. By default, asserts are turned on and it is recommended that users leave asserts on during development.
The HDMI RX driver is composed of several source files. This allows the user to build and link only those parts of the driver that are necessary.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 EB 02/05/19 Initial release.
